claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with Alex:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. That's very helpful. Just a few more questions to ensure I have all the information needed:

**Are you responsible for Alex's day-to-day care and expenses (such as food, clothes, and other needs)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am responsible for Alex's day-to-day care and all his expenses like food, clothes, and everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just one final question to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
✓ You live in the UK
✓ Alex lives with you
✓ You are responsible for his care and expenses
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim if you haven't already done so. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and as Alex's parent who is responsible for his care and lives with him, you qualify.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which means he is under 16 years old, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. Alex lives with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care and expenses including food, clothes, and other needs, which means the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, meaning there is no conflict with another claim. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex's care and expenses, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All the eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which means he is under 16 years old, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. Alex lives with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care and expenses including food, clothes, and other needs, which means the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, meaning there is no conflict with another claim. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex's care and expenses, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All the eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
